summary: Full regression checkpoint sweep across backend + frontend per review_request. Backend: 164/164 full pytest pass + 9/9 iter15 live-preview smoke (incl. P2 Save-to-Share, Orca status aarch64, voice command, public lists, billing packages). Frontend: workspace loads with zero console/page errors after session cookie injection, all 8 Add-Primitive buttons work, Boolean toolbar enables on multi-select, STL + 3MF exports both trigger downloads with ZERO React #321 errors (regression cleared), all 4 themes toggle without breakage, voice button toggles, Save-as-Component dialog opens with full form, Settings dialog reachable, Release Notes dialog displays v1.17.0 'Rigid-body rotations stay rigid · OrcaSlicer profile fix' on top as expected. Group-rotation P0 fix verified at math layer (rotation-group-consecutive.mjs, iter14) and UI controls are reachable. SKIPPED per request: actual Orca slicing CLI (aarch64 pod).

minor.issue: These endpoint paths listed in the review_request do NOT exist on the backend. Actual surfaces in use: /api/auth/session (Emergent session auth), /api/me/designs (projects), /api/components/{cid}/project (component fetch), /api/me/components (mine filter), /api/billing/packages + /api/billing/checkout + /api/billing/status/{id} (billing), /api/me/profile (settings), /api/slice/orca/reinstall (orca admin reinstall). All actual endpoints are exercised by existing pytest suite and all pass. No functional gap — just a naming mismatch in the review brief.

action_items: (Optional) Consider adding a data-testid for the Group/Combo button (currently no testid contains 'group'; uses 'COMBO' label). Helps future E2E group-rotation flows.
(Optional) Voice button does not expose aria-pressed for the listening state — using class-based styling instead. Adding aria-pressed would improve a11y + test selector ergonomics.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/backend/server.py is 1720+ lines — should be split into routers (auth, components, gallery, voice, billing, slice, admin, settings) for maintainability. Tracked in prior iterations.

Workspace gating now requires auth (Emergent session cookie). Anonymous workspace browse was previously possible — confirm with product if this is intentional. Public API endpoints (components list, gallery, billing/packages, printers, orca/status) remain anonymously accessible, which is correct.

Save-to-Share component POST accepts anonymous (no auth) submissions — verified: created TEST_PitmanArm_* component is publicly listed. If product requires auth-gated publishing, this is a policy gap. If intentional (open library), no action.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on exercised flows (primitives, exports, themes, dialogs, release notes)

test_credentials: Seeded session: user_id=user_iter15_1779985477797, session_token=st_iter15_1779985477797 (in test_database.user_sessions). Use cookie name 'session_token' on the preview domain. /app/memory/test_credentials.md present and used for context.
seed_data_creation: Inserted one test user + session into MongoDB test_database (users + user_sessions collections) to enable workspace UI testing. Test components created via API are TEST_* prefixed for easy cleanup.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is rock-solid (164/164 pytest). Workspace UI is now session-gated — inject 'session_token' cookie or use seeded session above. The P2 'Save-to-Share Pitman Arm' bug the user reported is NOT reproducible: POST /api/components with the full payload (name, author, category, modifier, stl_base64, project_json, triangle_count, object_count, private:false) succeeds and the component appears in GET /api/components. If user still sees the bug on prod, capture the exact request payload + response from the browser network tab — likely a frontend validation gate or missing required field on the client side, not a server bug. React #321 STL export regression is CLEAR (downloads work, zero errors). Group rotation P0 fix verified at math layer (rotation-group-consecutive.mjs passes per iter14). Orca aarch64 status returns installed:false as designed.
rca of the issue: No P0/P1 bugs found this iteration. P2 Save-to-Share path tested end-to-end on live preview URL and succeeds. The previously reported 'Pitman Arm didn't save to Share' is most likely a transient client-side issue (e.g. validation toast, network flake, or specific large-payload edge case) — could not be reproduced with anonymous or session-authed payloads up to ~12KB of stl_base64. Recommend the user reproduce on prod with DevTools Network tab open to capture the failing request.
